name: Sheresh Temporal Identity
work_cycles: Dome maintenance, heat discipline, and route timing clearly dominate Sheresh daily time even though the high-order calendar model remains unresolved
feast_days: Aurora observances and midwinter resilience festivals are active signals across source layers
legal_deadlines: Containment, loss reporting, and launch approvals clearly attach to time-sensitive survival windows regardless of calendar contradiction
shipping_windows: Cross-dome movement depends on weather judgment, contamination risk, and escorted route timing
weather_expectations: Axial flips, freeze severity, and aurora conditions shape public expectations more than abstract chronology alone
mourning_and_renewal_customs: Loss recording, survival remembrance, and communal endurance practices remain temporally embedded even without a safely closed civil calendar
public_language_about_time: People speak in route windows, dome strain, aurora conditions, and safe-day judgments; any civil calendric label must be contradiction-tagged
blocking_note: The civil structure conflict between the older 10-month/36-day/9-day-week model and later 9-month/40-day/7-day-week model remains unresolved and should not be flattened without explicit corrective evidence.
